changes.reason: Validate the dormant hard-risk module at a deliberately loose 12-point adverse threshold. This is not a realized stop-loss recommendation; it is a bounded risk-module experiment to determine whether the platform treats this value as a disabled/diagnostic adverse threshold or an active hard exit. Reject immediately if it creates realized-loss exits or suppresses the pullback/charm-pin recovery edge.

Run the stale-trade maintenance module as an isolated risk-family test. Latest tails are slow-to-profit recoveries with no >=12h holds, so the hypothesis is that stale-trade logic can trim pathological capital lockup without changing entry selection.

Enable trade-health validation to test whether existing health signals can distinguish the worst no-stop tail recoveries from normal profitable pullbacks. Prior entry/take-profit families are saturated and harmful; this tests runtime risk diagnostics instead of another entry prune.

expected_effect: Should reveal whether runtime risk modules improve drawdown, exposure hours, and worst/average MAE without starving trades. Reject if realized losses appear, net profit falls below 98% of run_001, trade count falls more than 10%, pnl_per_exposure_hour falls, or the module merely exits recoverable winners earlier.
label: runtime-risk-validation
rationale: Use batch_f_risk_module_validation because take-profit experiments are hard-saturated and recent call-wall/1DTE/fallback tightening hurt score and net profit. The latest run has no realized losses and no >=12h trades, but worst MAE deteriorated to -638.75 and several winners required long underwater recovery. This is the cleanest non-entry, non-TP experiment to test whether existing runtime health/stale logic can reduce hidden no-stop tail cost while preserving the profitable entry population.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: run_009 has 98 winners and zero realized losses, but worst MAE is -638.75, average MAE is about -106, exposure is 35.6 hours, and worst tails took 80-188 minutes to recover. Trade-health signals were logged as zero in the current run, so enabling the module is a direct validation of whether the switch is currently inert or genuinely protective.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Runtime Health Tail Containment
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The module may convert recoverable no-stop winners into premature realized losses or reduce net profit without improving score; reject on any meaningful profit suppression or realized-loss creation.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: Open long trade develops stale/poor health characteristics after entry rather than being filtered pre-entry.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Risk-adjusted score improves with net profit >=98% of run_001, pnl_per_exposure_hour flat or better, no material trade starvation, and worst MAE improving toward <= -527 without realized-loss proliferation.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: The latest run remains a favorable long-only momentum/pullback sample: 98 trades, 100% realized win rate, no slow >=12h trades, and 12.0% calendar exposure. However, it is below...

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Revival
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1298.7, PnL/exposure hour >170, average MAE improves versus breakout_long_1dte, and total leader score is not diluted.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Split Test
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: New dormant pin-avoid profile must be independently PnL positive, keep average MAE below active-profile median, and avoid reducing charm_pin/charm_wall combined PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Early Path Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE toward <= -527 or better while preserving >=95% total PnL and >=95% fast-winner count.
